attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- commit-optimizer: Create structured optimization commit messages with measured performance/size results. Use this whenever you're committing code optimizations (especially RISC-V libm assembly tuning) and want to automatically benchmark the changes and generate a properly formatted commit message with problem, approach, and result sections. Invoke with `/commit`.
- libm-testpoints: IEEE 754 numerical analysis expert for generating representative libm test points. Use this skill whenever the user asks to generate test points, test cases, or test inputs for math library (libm) functions (sin, cos, exp, log, sqrt, pow, etc.), whether float or double precision. Also trigger when the user wants to improve, audit, or extend existing test_*.c files in the RISC-V libm test harness. Trigger on requests like "generate 100 test points for sinf", "what are the key test cases for expf?", or "add representative tests to test_logf.c". The argument is the function name, e.g. "/libm-testpoints sinf" or "/libm-testpoints sin".
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions on AX45MPV (VLEN=1024). Runs a 7-step FSM — select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, profile, learn — then loops back to analyze with new pipeline insights. No human intervention required.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session

content.text:
`git checkout -- **/*.c` would discard all uncommitted changes in the modified .c files listed in git status (many Source/ConvolutionFunctions/ files). This is destructive and unrecoverable.

Confirm before I proceed, or clarify if you meant something else (e.g., list them, stage them, diff them).

content.content:
On branch develop
Your branch is behind 'origin/develop' by 26 commits, and can be fast-forwarded.
  (use "git pull" to update your local branch)

Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
  (use "git restore <file>..." to discard changes in working directory)
	modified:   .gitignore
	modified:   project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
	modified:   project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
	modified:   test_perf.sh

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	CLAUDE.md
	qemu-validate/

no changes added to commit (use "git add" and/or "git commit -a")
---
diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
index 4c2a9039..42099b46 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -6,4 +6,6 @@
 *.size
 *.map
 .vscode/
-*.db
\ No newline at end of file
+*.db
+.optdb/
+.claude/opt-state.json
\ No newline at end of file
diff --git a/project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp b/project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
index 9acbe9cb..f4e76f79 100755
--- a/project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
+++ b/project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp
@@ -10,7 +10,7 @@ set timeout 600
 # Setup the nds32 gdb full-path so that we can execute it to connect to aice.
 # This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
 #   e.g. "set gdb /path/to/nds32le-elf-newlib-v3/bin/nds32le-elf-gdb"
-set gdb riscv64-elf-gdb
+set gdb riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython
 
 # Setup the host machine that iceman is running on.
 # This value should be replaced by build system or manually changed.
diff --git a/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board b/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
index f7c1409b..f2a56f75 100755
--- a/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
+++ b/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board
@@ -53,7 +53,7 @@ GCC="no"
 CACHE="on"
 
 # set the board need reset-and-run or not.
-RESET_BOARD="on"
+RESET_BOARD="off"
 
 # setup the PMA for HVM (it could be "on" or "off")
 HVM_PMA="off"
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ DC_WA_THRESHOLD="1"
 
 # we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
 if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
-    RESET="on"
+    RESET="off"
     touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
 else
     RESET=$RESET_BOARD
diff --git a/test_perf.sh b/test_perf.sh
index f1cdfecd..111d3b2f 100755
--- a/test_perf.sh
+++ b/test_perf.sh
@@ -29,6 +29,8 @@
 #        - riscv64-elf-clang
 #  $3: the target to run the executables
 #      Now the target could only be BOARD.
+#  $4: (optional) single function name to test
+#      When specified, only this function is compiled and run.
 #
 # Example:
 #  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
@@ -43,16 +45,31 @@
 #  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
 #  and clang compiler
 #  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
+#
+#  ex-5: measure a single function with defaults (ax45mpv)
+#  ./test_perf.sh "" "" "" avepool_HWC_s8
 ###########################################################################
 
-CPU_NAME=${1,,}   ## always convert to lower case
-COMPILER_NAME="$2"
+##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
+AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
+if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
+    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
+fi
+export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
+export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1116}"
+
+CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
+CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
+COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
+
 if [ -z ${3} ]; then
     TARGET="BOARD"
 else
     TARGET="${3}"
 fi
 
+SINGLE_FUNC="$4"
+
 function log () {
     echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
 }
@@ -60,6 +77,7 @@ function log () {
 ##### misc settings #####
 LIB_NAME="libnn"
 LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
+rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
 TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
 ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
 
@@ -211,6 +229,20 @@ if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
     fi
 fi
 
+##### Link against source-built libnn #####
+# Use the library at the repo root (built via ./build.sh AX45MPV) instead of
+# the toolchain's built-in libnn_v.a.
+BUILD_LIB_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}"
+if [[ ! -f "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn.a" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: libnn.a not found at ${BUILD_LIB_DIR}. Run build.sh first."
+    exit 1
+fi
+# The linker flag -lnn_v expects libnn_v.a. build.sh produces libnn.a, so
+# create a symlink with the expected name.
+ln -sf "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn.a" "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn_v.a"
+# Prepend -L so the linker finds our library before the toolchain's built-in.
+CPU_FLAGS="-L${BUILD_LIB_DIR} ${CPU_FLAGS}"
+
 ##### main ######
 rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
 log "***************************************************************************************"
@@ -218,17 +250,73 @@ log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
 log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
 log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
 log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
+log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
 log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
 log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
+if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
+    log "SINGLE_FUNC: ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
+fi
 log "***************************************************************************************"
 
 EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
-${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
+
+if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
+    #####################################################################
+    # Single-function mode: compile and run one test directly
+    #####################################################################
+    UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
+    SRC_FILE="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.c"
+    if [[ ! -f "${SRC_FILE}" ]]; then
+        echo "Error: test source not found: ${SRC_FILE}"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    # Build flags matching what test.sh uses for BOARD + v-ext perf tests
+    APP_CFLAGS="-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -Wl,-gc-sections -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
+    INC_DIR="-I$(${COMPILER_NAME} -print-sysroot)/../../riscv64-elf/include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
+    ADX_FILE="t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.adx"
+
+    build_cmd="${COMPILER_NAME} ${APP_CFLAGS} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_FILE} ${SRC_FILE} ${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -lm"
+    log "Build: ${build_cmd}"
+    ${build_cmd} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
+    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]]; then
+        echo "Error: compilation failed for ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    log "Running ${SINGLE_FUNC} on board..."
+    log "Start at $(date)"
+    ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_FILE} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
+    ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
+    log "End at $(date)"
+
+    # Parse inst/cycle from output
+    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
+        inst=$(grep -w 'inst' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
+        cycle=$(grep -w 'cycle' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
+        log ""
+        log "Result: ${SINGLE_FUNC}  inst=${inst}  cycle=${cycle}"
+    else
+        log "Error: board execution failed (ret=${ret})"
+    fi
+else
+    #####################################################################
+    # Full test suite mode
+    #####################################################################
+    ${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
+fi
 
 log "***************************************************************************************"
 log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
 log "***************************************************************************************"
 
-# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
-out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
-mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
+# move the log into test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder (full mode only)
+if [[ -z "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
+    out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
+    mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
+fi
[rerun: b3]

 log "***************************************************************************************"
 
 EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
-${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
+
+if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
+    #####################################################################
+    # Single-function mode: compile and run one test directly
+    #####################################################################
+    UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
+    SRC_FILE="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.c"
+    if [[ ! -f "${SRC_FILE}" ]]; then
+        echo "Error: test source not found: ${SRC_FILE}"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    # Build flags matching what test.sh uses for BOARD + v-ext perf tests
+    APP_CFLAGS="-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -Wl,-gc-sections -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
+    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
+    INC_DIR="-I$(${COMPILER_NAME} -print-sysroot)/../../riscv64-elf/include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
+    ADX_FILE="t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.adx"
+
+    build_cmd="${COMPILER_NAME} ${APP_CFLAGS} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_FILE} ${SRC_FILE} ${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -lm"
+    log "Build: ${build_cmd}"
+    ${build_cmd} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
+    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]]; then
+        echo "Error: compilation failed for ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    log "Running ${SINGLE_FUNC} on board..."
+    log "Start at $(date)"
+    ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_FILE} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
+    ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
+    log "End at $(date)"
+
+    # Parse inst/cycle from output
+    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
+        inst=$(grep -w 'inst' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
+        cycle=$(grep -w 'cycle' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
+        log ""
+        log "Result: ${SINGLE_FUNC}  inst=${inst}  cycle=${cycle}"
+    else
+        log "Error: board execution failed (ret=${ret})"
+    fi
+else
+    #####################################################################
+    # Full test suite mode
+    #####################################################################
+    ${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
+fi
 
 log "***************************************************************************************"
 log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
 log "***************************************************************************************"
 
-# move the log int test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder
-out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
-mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
+# move the log into test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder (full mode only)
+if [[ -z "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
+    out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
+    mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
+fi
